sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Dano Finance's DeFi Kernel registry, Spot Leverage Order Book, American Options Market-Making protocol, and Composable DeFi Transaction Builder SDK.
Recipient: Dano Finance, with Minswap Labs serving as budget administrator

Deliverables: DeFi Kernel registry website and submission process, Spot Leverage Order Book, American Options Market-Making protocol, Composable DeFi Transaction Builder SDK, public documentation, reporting, and integration support

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,333,000 ADA total, consisting of 3,300,000 ADA for delivery and 33,000 ADA as a 1% budget administration fee for Minswap Labs.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Dano Finance says the DeFi Kernel will provide an open, fee-free, royalty-free compatibility standard and registry for Cardano DeFi contracts.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims Cardano DeFi is fragmented across liquidity, metadata, and execution paths, which slows integrations and limits advanced financial applications.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Dano Finance claims it currently has approximately USD 18M in TVL and more than 10,000 on-chain transactions.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The work is divided into four budgeted work packages: 300,000 ADA for the registry, 1,000,000 ADA for the Spot Leverage Order Book, 1,000,000 ADA for the American Options Protocol, and 1,000,000 ADA for the SDK.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says smart contract workstreams will include security review or audit before mainnet release and that unresolved critical security issues block affected mainnet milestones.
missing: Independent evidence for: Dano Finance says the DeFi Kernel will provide an open, fee-free, royalty-free compatibility standard and registry for Cardano DeFi contracts.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Cardano DeFi is fragmented across liquidity, metadata, and execution paths, which slows integrations and limits advanced financial applications.
Independent evidence for: Dano Finance claims it currently has approximately USD 18M in TVL and more than 10,000 on-chain transactions.
Independent evidence for: Dano Finance says unused or unearned funds will remain unspent or be returned to the Cardano Treasury according to the administrator's process.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,333,000 ADA total, consisting of 3,300,000 ADA for delivery and 33,000 ADA as a 1% budget administration fee for Minswap Labs." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Dano Finance says the DeFi Kernel will provide an open, fee-free, royalty-free compatibility standard and registry for Cardano DeFi contracts." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
